creator: jedgar@fxp.org
text:
On Sun, 3 Oct 1999, Will Andrews wrote:

> On 02-Oct-99 jedgar@fxp.org wrote:
> > XLIB_DEPENDS= gnome.2:${PORTSDIR}/x11/gnomelibs
> > X
> > XGLIB_CONFIG?=        ${LOCALBASE}/bin/glib12-config
> > XGTK_CONFIG?= ${X11BASE}/bin/gtk12-config
> 
> I couldn't help but notice that you seem to have forgotten:
> LIB_DEPENDS+=   gtk12.2:${PORTSDIR}/x11-toolkits/gtk12
> 
> HOWEVER, exploring the gnomelibs dependencies does reveal a LIB_DEPENDS for gtk
> under imlib. STILL.. is this program compilable without GNOME? If it is, GTK
> will need to be in LIB_DEPENDS.

Yes, it requires GNOME; that is why other dependencies are not present.
This is also done by other GNOME ports such as balsa, gnomepim, genius,
gide, etc so I thought it best to follow suit.

-----
Chris D. Faulhaber <jedgar@fxp.org>  |  All the true gurus I've met never
System/Network Administrator,        |  claimed they were one, and always
Reality Check Information, Inc.      |  pointed to someone better.
